items.title: Adriana Rivas, the Bondi nanny accused of torture for Pinochet, makes last stand to evade extradition to Chile

items.summary: While markets struggle to price a sudden collapse in oil supply, military analysts are sounding the alarm on the rate countries are burning through weapons stockpiles.

items.summary: The deal comes at a crucial time for Kyiv, as US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has said weapons for Ukraine could be diverted to the Iran war

items.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show after mother's disappearance
items.summary: Savannah Guthrie is scheduled to return to her job as a co-anchor of NBC's Today show on April 6, more than two months after her 84-year-old mother's unresolved abduction.

items.title: Socceroos snatch late win against Cameroon as Jordy Bos delivers and teenager Lucas Herrington impresses
items.summary: Jordy Bos hits the back of the net as the Socceroos leave it late against Cameroon in Sydney with 18-year-old Lucas Herrington impressing on debut.

items.title: Police called to Canberra Centre after reports of teenagers brandishing knives, hammer
items.summary: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

items.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries
items.summary: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

items.summary: The new spirit of Tasmania ferries might not be available for journeys across the Bass Strait yet — but this winter, one of them will take on a new role as an art gallery, during Hobart's Dark Mofo festival.

items.summary: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

items.summary: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

items.title: Gladstone's Jake Lyle to star in Phantom of the Opera on Sydney Harbour

items.summary: The national peak body for aged care providers is calling on the federal government to provide urgent support to keep services running, as the Middle East war sends fuel prices skyrocketing.

items.title: Enriched uranium hidden deep in Iranian mountains could be key to ending war

items.title: 'QuitGPT' boycott could spark AI flashpoint as ethical use debate rages
items.summary: AI companies are stuck in a "perfect storm" of trying to control the use and output of their platforms while maintaining competitive profits, as millions of people boycott the technology, experts say.

items.title: Big Red Bash cancelled for 2026, outback tourism facing uncertain season as fuel prices bite
items.summary: This festival usually sees people go all out, spending thousands of dollars and travelling thousands of kilometres, but a potential change in location and fuel concerns have deterred ticket holders.

items.title: Petition calls for release of police video of Indigenous man's death in custody
items.summary: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.

items.title: Max Verstappen kicks Guardian F1 reporter out of Japanese Grand Prix press conference
items.summary: Max Verstappen refuses to speak to media until Guardian journalist Giles Richards leaves because of a question he asked last year.

items.title: Mudgee Medical Centre secures six doctors to reopen books after 18 months

items.title: ASX shares in lacklustre territory as markets focus on Middle East war duration — as it happened
items.summary: Australian shares finished broadly flat today. The Australian dollar managed to breach 69 US cents, but trading was lacklustre. For more on all of the ramifications, see how the day unfolded on our business blog.

items.title: Almost one in three eligible voters in Coober Pedy were unable to cast a ballot
items.summary: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

items.title: Daly Cherry-Evans's return to Brookvale Oval a look into the past as Manly faces uncertain future
items.summary: With Manly's season teetering on the edge, the Sea Eagles faithful welcomed former skipper Daly Cherry-Evans to Brookvale Oval in enemy colours for the first time. But it was the prodigal son who won the day.

items.title: Male victim-survivors of childhood sexual abuse overrepresented in suicide statistics, but seldom spoken about
items.summary: Craig Hughes-Cashmore spent more than 20 years living with persistent suicidal thoughts, an experience that many share but rarely feel able to discuss.

items.title: Police mark 40th anniversary of Melbourne's Russell Street bombing
items.summary: The 1986 car bomb attack on Victoria Police headquarters in Melbourne claimed the life of 21-year-old constable Angela Taylor and injured more than 20 others.

items.title: Advocates call for funding of transplant strategy to arrest declining organ donation rate
items.summary: Vanessa Hujber did not think twice before donating her kidney to save the life of her daughter, Amelia. But Amelia was one of the lucky ones, with advocates calling for urgent action to arrest Australia's declining living organ donation rate.

items.title: Vanished: Six months of searching for Gus Lamont
items.summary: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

items.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran

items.title: How 'traditional' gender roles are making a comeback in modern society
items.summary: It turns out generation Z has some of the strongest traditional beliefs about gender roles. The revival of what many see as outdated gender norms is being attributed to "manosphere" content on social media.

items.title: Are the US and Iran about to reach a ceasefire deal?
items.summary: Donald Trump says Iran wants a ceasefire deal. But Tehran says it's not even talking with the US. So what's going on and how likely is a ceasefire?

items.summary: The war in Iran renews the government's interest in reducing Australia's dependence on imported packaging, particularly oil-reliant plastic.

items.title: Medical drama Grey's Anatomy marks 21 years of love, loss and disasters

items.title: Review of Tasmania's climate laws finds net zero status could be 'temporary'
